claims.name: Default to
claims.statement: Per-non-default-persona cell table. `classification_r` = the default-persona ridge probe (Gemma-3-27B, L32, end-of-turn selector) predicting that persona's held-out Thurstonian utilities on the 1k canonical test split. `utility_similarity_r` = Pearson r between default and that persona's utilities on the same tasks. `probe_vs_utility_gap` = classification_r − utility_similarity_r, the bonus the default probe offers over the utility-similarity baseline.
claims.derivation: Per persona p: `transfer_r[default_idx, p_idx]` (from transfer_tb-5_L32.npz), `utility_r[default_idx, p_idx]` (from utility_similarity.npz), and their difference; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Default probe classification r minimum across personas
claims.statement: Minimum across the six non-default personas of the default probe's Pearson r predicting that persona's held-out Thurstonian utilities (eot, L32).
claims.derivation: min over non-default personas of `transfer_r[default_idx, j]`; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Default probe classification r maximum across personas
claims.statement: Maximum across the six non-default personas of the default probe's Pearson r predicting that persona's held-out Thurstonian utilities (eot, L32).
claims.derivation: max over non-default personas of `transfer_r[default_idx, j]`; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Default probe gap minimum across personas
claims.statement: Minimum across the six non-default personas of the probe-minus-utility delta — the smallest bonus the default probe offers over the utility-similarity baseline.
claims.derivation: min over non-default personas of (`transfer_r[default_idx, j]` − `utility_r[default_idx, j]`); round to 3dp.

claims.name: Default probe gap maximum across personas
claims.statement: Maximum across the six non-default personas of the probe-minus-utility delta — the largest bonus the default probe offers over the utility-similarity baseline.
claims.derivation: max over non-default personas of (`transfer_r[default_idx, j]` − `utility_r[default_idx, j]`); round to 3dp.

claims.name: Persona-transfer diagonal r minimum
claims.statement: Minimum across the 7 persona self-fit cells of the probe's held-out Pearson r — attained by contrarian (eot, L32).
claims.derivation: min over personas of the diagonal `transfer_r[i, i]` (self-fit); round to 3dp.

claims.name: Persona-transfer diagonal r minimum persona
claims.statement: Persona achieving the minimum self-fit Pearson r in the 7x7 transfer matrix (eot, L32).
claims.derivation: argmin over personas of the diagonal `transfer_r[i, i]`.

claims.name: Persona-transfer diagonal r maximum
claims.statement: Maximum across the 7 persona self-fit cells of the probe's held-out Pearson r — attained by mathematician (eot, L32).
claims.derivation: max over personas of the diagonal `transfer_r[i, i]`; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Persona-transfer diagonal r maximum persona
claims.statement: Persona achieving the maximum self-fit Pearson r in the 7x7 transfer matrix (eot, L32).
claims.derivation: argmax over personas of the diagonal `transfer_r[i, i]`.

claims.name: Persona-transfer off-diagonal mean r
claims.statement: Mean probe transfer Pearson r across the 42 ordered off-diagonal (train, eval) persona pairs at eot / L32.
claims.derivation: mean of off-diagonal cells (i != j) of `transfer_r`; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Persona-transfer positive-bonus pair count
claims.statement: Number of ordered off-diagonal (train, eval) persona pairs (out of 42) for which probe transfer r strictly exceeds the utility-utility r between the two personas (eot, L32).
claims.derivation: count of off-diagonal cells where (`transfer_r` − `utility_r`) > 0.

claims.name: Persona-transfer off-diagonal pair count
claims.statement: Number of ordered off-diagonal (train, eval) persona pairs used to summarise transfer (7 personas, self-pairs excluded).
claims.derivation: count of off-diagonal cells of `transfer_r` (7*7 − 7 = 42).

claims.name: Persona
claims.statement: Per-persona layer-averaged transfer summary (eot selector, layers {25, 32, 39, 46, 53}). `mean_outbound_r_across_layers` = average Pearson r of this persona's probe evaluated on each of the other six personas (6 off-diagonal pairs per layer, then mean across layers). `mean_inbound_r_across_layers` = same with the roles reversed (other probes → this persona).
claims.derivation: For each persona p and each layer L, take the mean of off-diagonal transfer_r rows (outbound) and columns (inbound) from transfer_tb-5_L{L}.npz; then mean across the 5 layers; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Transfer asymmetry maximum absolute gap
claims.statement: Maximum absolute difference |r(A→B) − r(B→A)| across the 21 unordered persona pairs at eot, layer 32.
claims.derivation: max over the 21 unordered persona pairs (i < j) of |`transfer_r[i,j]` − `transfer_r[j,i]`|; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Transfer asymmetry mean absolute gap
claims.statement: Mean absolute difference |r(A→B) − r(B→A)| across the 21 unordered persona pairs at eot, layer 32.
claims.derivation: mean over the 21 unordered persona pairs of |`transfer_r[i,j]` − `transfer_r[j,i]`|; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Transfer asymmetry median absolute gap
claims.statement: Median absolute difference |r(A→B) − r(B→A)| across the 21 unordered persona pairs at eot, layer 32 — quoted in the caption of the asymmetry figure.
claims.derivation: median over the 21 unordered persona pairs of |`transfer_r[i,j]` − `transfer_r[j,i]`|; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Mean off-diagonal transfer r at L
claims.statement: Mean Pearson r across the 42 ordered off-diagonal (train, eval) persona pairs at the end-of-turn selector, per layer.
claims.derivation: For each layer, mean of off-diagonal cells (i != j) of `transfer_r` from the corresponding transfer_tb-5_L{layer}.npz; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Corr bias raw mean r pred-train
claims.statement: Mean raw Pearson r between the probe's prediction vector and the training persona's Thurstonian utilities, averaged over the 30 ordered non-default (T, E) pairs with T != E and both non-default (eot, L32).
claims.derivation: mean of `r_train[i,j]` over ordered pairs (i, j) with i != j and neither i nor j == default_idx; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Corr bias raw mean r pred-default
claims.statement: Mean raw Pearson r between the probe's prediction vector and the default assistant's Thurstonian utilities, averaged over the 30 ordered non-default (T, E) pairs (eot, L32).
claims.derivation: mean of `r_default[i,j]` over the same 30 non-default (T, E) pairs; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Corr bias raw pairs on train-favouring side
claims.statement: Count of non-default (T, E) pairs (out of 30) for which the raw r(pred, train) exceeds r(pred, default) — pairs lying below the y = x diagonal in the left panel of the corr-bias figure.
claims.derivation: count of the 30 non-default (T, E) pairs with `r_train[i,j]` > `r_default[i,j]`.

claims.name: Corr bias non-default pair count
claims.statement: Number of ordered (T, E) persona pairs with T != E and both non-default, used in both panels of the corr-bias figure.
claims.derivation: count of cells with i != j and neither == default_idx in the 7x7 matrices (6*5 = 30).

claims.name: Corr bias partial mean r pred-train given eval
claims.statement: Mean partial Pearson r(pred, train | eval) across the 30 non-default (T, E) pairs at eot, L32: the probe's residual alignment with the training persona after regressing out the eval persona's utilities.
claims.derivation: mean of `r_train_partial[i,j]` over the 30 non-default (T, E) pairs; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Corr bias partial mean r pred-default given eval
claims.statement: Mean partial Pearson r(pred, default | eval) across the 30 non-default (T, E) pairs at eot, L32: the probe's residual alignment with the default assistant after regressing out the eval persona's utilities.
claims.derivation: mean of `r_default_partial[i,j]` over the 30 non-default (T, E) pairs; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Corr bias partial pairs on train-favouring side
claims.statement: Count of non-default (T, E) pairs (out of 30) for which the partial r(pred, train | eval) exceeds the partial r(pred, default | eval) — the '30/30' unanimity quoted in the caption and prose.
claims.derivation: count of the 30 non-default (T, E) pairs with `r_train_partial[i,j]` > `r_default_partial[i,j]`.

claims.name: Double-partial bias toward mathematician
claims.statement: Mean double-partial Pearson r(pred, u_mathematician | u_E, u_T) across the 30 ordered (T, E) non-default persona pairs at eot, L32: the probe prediction's residual alignment with mathematician's utilities after controlling for both the eval and train personas' utilities.
claims.derivation: nanmean of `bias_toward_mathematician` across the 30 ordered non-default (T, E) pairs; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Double-partial bias toward default
claims.statement: Mean double-partial Pearson r(pred, u_default | u_E, u_T) across the 30 ordered (T, E) non-default persona pairs at eot, L32: the probe prediction's residual alignment with default's utilities after controlling for both the eval and train personas' utilities.
claims.derivation: nanmean of `bias_toward_default` across the 30 ordered non-default (T, E) pairs; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Double-partial bias toward strategist
claims.statement: Mean double-partial Pearson r(pred, u_strategist | u_E, u_T) across the 30 ordered (T, E) non-default persona pairs at eot, L32: the probe prediction's residual alignment with strategist's utilities after controlling for both the eval and train personas' utilities.
claims.derivation: nanmean of `bias_toward_strategist` across the 30 ordered non-default (T, E) pairs; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Double-partial bias toward aura
claims.statement: Mean double-partial Pearson r(pred, u_aura | u_E, u_T) across the 30 ordered (T, E) non-default persona pairs at eot, L32: the probe prediction's residual alignment with aura's utilities after controlling for both the eval and train personas' utilities.
claims.derivation: nanmean of `bias_toward_aura` across the 30 ordered non-default (T, E) pairs; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Double-partial bias toward contrarian
claims.statement: Mean double-partial Pearson r(pred, u_contrarian | u_E, u_T) across the 30 ordered (T, E) non-default persona pairs at eot, L32: the probe prediction's residual alignment with contrarian's utilities after controlling for both the eval and train personas' utilities.
claims.derivation: nanmean of `bias_toward_contrarian` across the 30 ordered non-default (T, E) pairs; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Double-partial bias toward slacker
claims.statement: Mean double-partial Pearson r(pred, u_slacker | u_E, u_T) across the 30 ordered (T, E) non-default persona pairs at eot, L32: the probe prediction's residual alignment with slacker's utilities after controlling for both the eval and train personas' utilities.
claims.derivation: nanmean of `bias_toward_slacker` across the 30 ordered non-default (T, E) pairs; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Double-partial bias toward sadist
claims.statement: Mean double-partial Pearson r(pred, u_sadist | u_E, u_T) across the 30 ordered (T, E) non-default persona pairs at eot, L32: the probe prediction's residual alignment with sadist's utilities after controlling for both the eval and train personas' utilities.
claims.derivation: nanmean of `bias_toward_sadist` across the 30 ordered non-default (T, E) pairs; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Train self-bias partial r pred-train given eval
claims.statement: Mean partial r(pred, u_T | u_E) across all 42 ordered off-diagonal (T, E) persona pairs at eot, L32 — the green dashed reference line in the full-bias figure; the 'partial r = 0.67' quoted in the main-text paragraph on probe bias.
claims.derivation: nanmean of `train_self_bias` across the 42 ordered off-diagonal (T, E) pairs; round to 3dp.
